target_emits.name: create_gateway_cluster
target_emits.purpose: Create the HA gateway-cluster profile — the object that owns the explicit member gateways + their CoA-VRRP IPs (and multicast-vlan / heartbeat). Always emitted: required on every strategy because it carries CoA + membership. Created LOCAL at the target scope via query params.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST per source cluster_prof record.

target_emits.name: create_gw_cluster_intent
target_emits.purpose: Create the gateway-cluster INTENT config (the modern path). Declares cluster-mode (CM_SITE / CM_MANUAL) + device-type. Gated by emit_when: only fires for the intent_site / intent_manual strategies (skipped for ha_only). Scope placement is operator-driven via central_scope_id (CM_SITE → global/site/site-collection; CM_MANUAL → device-group).
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST when the strategy is intent_site / intent_manual; suppressed for ha_only.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: Gateway clusters terminate AP tunnels — always MOBILITY_GW for the AOS 8 → Central migration.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id is supplied by the consuming skill at runtime. The strategy determines the appropriate breadth: ha_only / intent_manual → device-group; intent_site → global / site / site-collection.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Operator gateway-topology answer + the source cluster's binding scope

dependencies.depends_on: (none — foundational; the cluster is created before the SSIDs that tunnel to it)

depended_by.relation: a tunneled / hybrid wlan-ssid binds to this cluster via the overlay-wlan object
depended_by.notes: Run gateway_cluster BEFORE the tunneled wlan_ssid + its overlay-wlan binding. One SSID may bind to multiple clusters (gw-cluster-list is an array).

objects.notes: REST schema 'cluster_prof' (CLI: lc-cluster group-profile). cluster_controller[] holds the member controllers (ip + vrrp_ip = CoA-VRRP IP + prio + mcast_vlan/vrrp_vlan + group_id). Live-verified on the tenant ('East' 2-node, VRRP-150) and the North_Cluster MD config (10 nodes, redundancy groups 1/2).

ipv4_gateways.notes: cluster_controller[] -> [{ip, coa-vrrp-ip=vrrp_ip, priority=prio}]. Substituted whole into the body. Member-inherited entries are NOT dropped.

emit_intent.notes: True for intent_site / intent_manual; absent for ha_only. Gates the gw-cluster-intent-config emit.

unmapped_fields.from: redundancy (active/active vs active/standby), ap_load_balancing, the rebalance thresholds (active_ap_rebal_*, unbalance_threshold, act_cl_rebal_act_load_perc, sby_cl_rebal_tot_load_perc), vrrp_info (vrrp_id/passphrase), cluster_controller[].group_id / vrrp_vlan / rap_public_ip, cluster_prof_clone, _flags
unmapped_fields.reason: v1 maps the membership + CoA + multicast + heartbeat core. redundancy → Central 'one-to-one-redundancy' is deferred pending a live semantic confirm (AOS active/active vs active/standby vs Central's one-to-one flag). Rebalance/load-balancing thresholds, the VRRP group id/passphrase, and per-member group_id/vrrp_vlan/rap_public_ip don't have confirmed Central counterparts on gateway-clusters yet. clone is a CLI convenience.

ignored_variants.reason: Consumer filters system/default cluster records at the record level before emit_calls.

draft_notes: v1 translation. Dual-object Central clustering: gateway-clusters (HA, always) + gw-cluster-intent-config (intent, gated by cluster_strategy via emit_when). Created LOCAL at the target scope via query params (object-type=LOCAL / scope-id / device-function=MOBILITY_GW) — clusters use direct scope-creation, NOT the SHARED-create-then-config-assignment pattern the profile translations use.

STRATEGY FORK (runtime_values['cluster_strategy']): ha_only → step 1 only; intent_site → step 1 + step 2 CM_SITE; intent_manual → step 1 + step 2 CM_MANUAL. The skill picks per-cluster from the operator's gateway-topology answer (no-DMZ → intent_site@global; campus+DMZ → intent_site@site for campus + intent_manual@device-group for DMZ; DMZ-only → ha_only).
DEPENDENCY: depended_by central:wlan_ssid — tunneled/hybrid SSIDs bind to this cluster via the overlay-wlan gw-cluster-list. Run gateway_cluster first.

NEEDS-LIVE-VERIFY (write-path, not preview): the object-type=LOCAL vs SHARED choice + global-scope scope-id semantics for CM_SITE@global; the redundancy → one-to-one-redundancy mapping; whether multicast-vlan should come from mcast_vlan or vrrp_vlan. Preview is read-only so these don't block design validation.
Idempotency: SHARED/LOCAL POST for an existing name returns HTTP 409; consumer handles 409 idempotently.
